metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_1501
metadata.comment:
Rationale: The authors use synaptosomes prepared from rat cerebellum, to show that 5-HT treatment reduces K+-induced glutamate release, and this inhibition can be relieved with the application of 5-HT1D antagonists.

21/11/2017 Pim
- In this paper the effect of metabotropic 5HT1 receptors is studied on two phenomena observed in synaptosomal preparations:
1] glutamate release, presynaptic phenomenon triggered by K+
2] cGMP production, postsynaptic phenomemon triggered by NMDA/AMPA-R activation caused by 1]
A. Serotonin and other serotonergic agonists inhibit 1] and 2]
B. A. is blocked by a 5HT1D antagonist (GR-127935)
C. Direct activation of NMDA/AMPA-Rs leads to varying efficiency of different Serotonin and other serotonergic agonists on phenomenon 2]. Together with A. this shows that 1] and 2] are functionally related (Glut release causes GlutR activation and cGMP production) and that different 5HT1Rs subtypes regulate 1] and 2]
D. 5HT1A antagonist ([(+)-WAY100135]) can rescue inhibition of 2] but not of 1] by serotonergic agonists
Conclusion:
- 5HT1D regulates presynaptic glutamate release
- 5HT1A regulates postsynaptic cGMP production

- This conclusion on 5HT1D is confirmed in study PMID:16469416 that concludes:
"The major finding of our work was that, while the cGMP production evoked by high-micromolar SNAP was insensitive to 5-HT, the cGMP response to NO donor concentrations releasing NO in the low-nanomolar range, was dependent on the release of glutamate and on subsequent activation of glutamate ionotropic receptors/NOS/sGC pathway and could be controlled by activating presynaptic 5-HT receptors of the 5-HT1D subtype."

- The 5HT1A-R clearly regulates cGMP production in the postsynapse (NMDA/AMPAR dependent).. Experimental description: 20/11/2017 Pim
- Tissue was of rat species; "Adult male rats (Sprague-Dawley, weighing 200-250 g) were housed at constanttemperature ..."
